reason: Both answers correctly say no, identify the deploy pipeline as the real bottleneck, and prescribe similar fixes (faster CI, decoupling deploy from release via feature flags, continuous deployment), cite Shopify/GitHub as counterexamples, and recommend modular monolith (Packwerk) as an intermediate step. They are very close in quality and correctness. The distinguishing point is the extract-only-along-real-seams criterion: Answer B explicitly closes with guidance to revisit microservices only when a component has genuinely different scaling or ownership needs, directly nailing that point, while Answer A only vaguely gestures at it. B also names a merge queue as a concrete tooling fix that A omits. Both are concise and correct, but B covers the key points slightly more precisely and completely.
